mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in real estate construction and consumer credit growth indicates a broad demand shock that compresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when both proxies decline concurrently, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial output, supply bottlenecks amplify price stickiness, allowing equities to maintain valuation multiples and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO falls below 98.0 during supply-constrained periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when industrial production exceeds 105.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent positive drift in equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day correlation between quarterly GDP growth revisions and weekly SPX returns remains below 0.10, or forward returns show no statistically significant positive drift following GDP upgrades.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital exceeds the historical earnings growth rate, forcing multiple compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand or remain stable when FEDFUNDS sustains levels above 4.5%, or the signal's hit rate for negative returns falls below 45% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns but SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction coefficient loses statistical significance in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, rapid rebounds in housing starts lead equity markets to price in accelerated consumer durables demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when HOUST rebounds by > 10% month-over-month during post-shock periods, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 2-year walk-forward test.
